title: Phenacetin - Canada.ca
content:
This substance was identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP).
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities
Final screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Acetamide, N-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-
Significant New Activity (SNAc) provisions

## Follow-up activities
While exposure of the  general population to phenacetin is not of concern at current levels, this  substance is considered to have a health effect of concern due to its potential  to cause cancer. Therefore, there may be a concern for human health if exposures  were to increase. An order amending the Domestic Substances List (DSL) to apply the SNAc provisions of the Canadian Environmental  Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999) to phenacetin was published.
Publication in the Canada    Gazette , Part II: Vol. 154, No. 15 of the order amending the DSL to apply the SNAc provisions of CEPA 1999.
Publication in  the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 30 of the notice of intent to amend the DSL to apply the SNAc provisions of CEPA 1999, and start of 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the  final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 30 .
Publication and start  of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment (including  follow-up activities). The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette, Part I: Vol. 151, No. 15 .
